content:
BDO

BDO is a leading provider of professional services to clients of all sizes in virtually all business sectors. We have proudly served the Edmonton community for more than 25 years, providing our clients with trusted advice in a number of areas including assurance & accounting, tax planning, SR&ED, financial advisory, business transitions, IT Solutions and more.

No matter your personal or business needs, our professionals can help. We focus on delivering personalized service and building strong relationships as we work to minimize risk and save you money.

We’re also big believers in giving back to the community and actively participate in a number of charities including the United Way, Edmonton Food Bank, Habitat for Humanity, Operation Friendship Seniors Society, Tools for School, Bottles for Winn, Big Brothers & Big Sisters, and Farm Credit Canada’s Drive Away Hunger.

Though we can’t help improve your straight drive, we can provide a range of professional services that will simplify your life. For more information, please visit us online at www.bdo.ca/edmonton

Our link to BDO is through ESC member Cody Hladki. You’ll often find him on the courts in the evening playing box-league, Interclub and Summer league. Cody started playing squash competitively 3 years ago, and it since has become a passion for him. Cody is always available for a pick-up match! He also loves to talk business, and would be more than happy to answer any questions you may have both on and off the court.

Cody recently joined the BDO team as a Manager in 2016. His focus is in assurance and advisory for small to mid-sized private companies, oil and gas, and not-for-profit organizations.

Prior to joining BDO, Cody specialized in large public and private company financial statement audits, personal and corporate tax compliance and planning, and internal processes and controls advisory.

His industry experience includes oil and gas, alternative energy production, IT, transportation and not-for-profit organizations.

Come visit BDO at 34th Ave and Parsons Road, or call 780-461-8000
